subject: rtl8821au: disable warnings.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: 3.6 Class E — Realtek USB WiFi (groups rtl8821au vendors/resyncs but does not list this commit)

impact.effect_if_absent: compiler warnings (-Wcast-function-type, -Wenum-conversion) during rtl8821au build — cosmetic, no functional effect

superseded_by: rtl8821au-morrownr (out-of-tree BR2 package, commit-pinned to 3a7cdb5, includes identical warning suppressions in current Makefile)

notes: rtl8821au was vendored in the Linux-Kernel_MiSTer fork but is NOT present in vanilla 6.18.38. It has been extracted to an out-of-tree Buildroot package (rtl8821au-morrownr, sourced from morrownr's actively-maintained fork) in P3.1. The warning suppressions (-Wno-cast-function-type, -Wno-enum-conversion) added here are already present in the current morrownr upstream Makefile (verified via git clone of morrownr/8821au-20210708 at HEAD). This commit is a follow-up compiler-flag fix applied during the kernel fork's active development but is not carried as a separate patch since the driver is no longer vendored. The disposition is dropped-obsolete: the code path no longer exists in our build, having been superseded by the re-sourced out-of-tree driver which independently maintains equivalent warning suppressions. No functional regression from dropping this: the out-of-tree driver handles its own build warnings.
